description_long: This is a weathered, rectangular stone block with a rough, irregular surface and visible signs of erosion and chipping. The block features three large, rectangular cavities running through its length, suggesting it is a hollow-core design often used in construction. The exterior is predominantly a pale, off-white color with patches of brownish and greenish discoloration, likely from exposure to the elements. One side is notably more eroded, with jagged edges and a rugged texture, while the other sides are relatively flat but still uneven. The block appears to be made primarily of concrete or a similar composite stone material, with air pockets visible in the hollow sections. Its substantial mass and solid build indicate it is intended as a structural or barrier element in an urban environment.
description: A weathered, hollow-core stone block with rough, chipped surfaces and three large rectangular cavities. The block is mostly off-white with patches of discoloration and signs of erosion.
description_view_0: The block is seen from a short end, highlighting the rough, jagged, and chipped surface with visible cavities inside.
description_view_1: This side shows a relatively flat, broad face with subtle vertical lines and less visible erosion compared to other views.
description_view_2: The opposite short end reveals a more intact but still rough surface, with the hollow interior partially visible.
description_view_3: The long side displays the three deep cavities and pronounced weathering along the edges, with some discoloration and surface roughness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Significant chipping, rough edges, and surface discoloration from exposure to the elements. Some greenish patches suggest mild moss or algae growth.
